title: story-16-review-churn
description:
This is a continuation of story-16-review-churn. Story 16 became active again in the current window with another successful dev run followed by another successful reviewer run, increasing cumulative reviewer cycles to 11 while remaining active after 2026-05-28T23:47:20.840537+00:00. The pattern is not a failure cluster: both runs succeeded, but the story is still cycling between dev and reviewer well past the expected convergence ceiling.

The immediate impact is continued cost and wall-clock burn without evidence of advancement. In this window alone, the dev run lasted 167.739s and cost $0.2958545, followed by a reviewer run costing $0.03447; the watcher note explicitly identifies this as churn continuing from the previously escalated 10-cycle state. Given the prior concern history already reached halt for the same story, this window strengthens that concern rather than introducing a new ambiguous signal.

A separate signal is that a new worktree was created for story 55 while the watcher note reports the missing-DB worktree set has grown to include stories 52, 53, and now 55. That orphan-growth observation is weaker than the review-churn evidence in this bundle because the underlying window only shows the creation event, not the DB lookup itself, so it should be treated as secondary context rather than the primary concern.

evidence.summary_excerpt: Story 16 became reviewer-active again in this window: a long successful dev run was followed by another successful reviewer run, increasing its cumulative review churn to 11 cycles.

evidence.excerpt: {"noteworthy":"Story 16 is active in this window with reviewer_cycles=11, up from the previously escalated 10, so the non-converging review loop is continuing rather than resolving."}

{"noteworthy":"A new worktree for story 55 now has db_state=\"missing\", adding to the existing missing-DB worktrees for stories 52 and 53; this suggests the orphaned-worktree set is growing."}

escalation_reason: Story 16 remains in a non-converging dev-review loop and has now persisted to 11 reviewer cycles while still active in the current interval. This pattern has continued across multiple prior concerns and now extends the already-halted condition with additional time and cost burn, so L3 judgment is needed on the sustained convergence failure.
